key_insight: Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS) uses prior-guided symbolic programs plus optimization to recover or discover interpretable PDE structure instead of only numerical values or neural approximations.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical route to automated, human-readable PDE characterizations that can support analysis, model discovery, and faster scientific insight in computational engineering workflows.
method: An agent encodes PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs.
Evolutionary search refines the symbolic form while gradient-based optimization fits continuous parameters.
The framework acts as inductive-bias injection, aiming for analytical expressions that are testable and interpretable.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on five PDE problems, where ASYS recovered known analytic forms and produced new interpretable formulas such as an Allen-Cahn interface law and a Keller-Segel contraction law.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on the quality of priors, search space design, and optimization stability, so results may be sensitive to problem-specific setup and not guarantee closed forms for all PDEs.
paper_title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
